title: Why Where You Are Is the Only Starting Place That Works
subtitle: A reading on the body, the place you actually live, and why the plan that begins there is the plan that lasts
excerpt: A ten-minute reading on why every workable body plan starts from where you actually are, not where you wish you were. The plan that begins with the body in the mirror is the plan that lasts because it is the only one that is true on day one.
sourceNotes: Original to homemade.education. The starting-from-where-you-are stance is shared across self-compassion teaching, recovery work, and Health at Every Size literature.

content.text: Most body plans fail in the first week. The reason is the same most of the time. The plan was made for a body that does not exist yet.

The plan assumed a body that already had the energy, the time, the sleep, the routine, and the steady mood that the plan was meant to produce. The plan was the destination, drawn as the starting line.
Where you are is the only place a plan can start
Where you are is the body you woke up in this morning. The hours of sleep you actually got. The food in the cupboard. The energy you actually have. The week you actually have in front of you.
A plan that starts here can be done today. A plan that starts somewhere else has to wait until you become a different person.
The wish-body plan

The wish-body plan is the one drawn for the body the wish-version of you would have. The wish-version sleeps eight hours. She loves vegetables. She walks every morning. She does not need a chocolate biscuit at 4pm. She does the workout even on the days the period is heavy.
She is not you on Monday morning. The plan she would follow is not the plan that works for you on Monday morning.
The where-you-are plan

The where-you-are plan starts with a small list. What you actually ate yesterday. How you actually slept. What kind of week is in front of you. What energy you have, on this morning, in this body, at this point in your cycle.
From that list, one move. One change you can make today, in this body, with this week. One change that the day can carry. That is the plan.
Why fast change rarely sticks

Fast change runs on a borrowed self. It runs on the early-week motivation. It runs on the version of you that watched the documentary at the weekend and decided everything would be different from Monday.
The borrowed self runs out somewhere around Wednesday. The body that has to do the new plan on Thursday is the body you already had. It just has more disappointment to work with now.

Slow change runs on the actual self. Slow change does not need motivation because it does not ask for more than the actual self can give. Slow change holds on Thursday because Thursday looks like Monday.
What this looks like in a week

In a week, the where-you-are plan looks small. One walk you actually took. One meal you actually ate at the table. One night you went to bed at a sensible time. One conversation with the body that was gentler than the last one.
It does not look impressive on day seven. It looks like the same body, with one or two things shifted. That is what a real plan looks like in a week.
In a month, the body has a few more of those days under it. In six months, the days are the body. There is no other path.
Where this reading comes from

Original to homemade.education. The where-you-are stance is shared across self-compassion teaching, across recovery work, and across Health at Every Size writing. The form here is written for the Homemade reader.
